what: Simple utilities for working in targets
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: collection utility functions interactively working targets pipelines. functions streamline common workflows like loading target outputs, running specific targets, handling spatial data.

title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: can install development version targets.utils GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("geryan/targets.utils")

title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: functions make interactive work targets pipelines faster: tl(): Load globals targets single command tml(x): Make load specific target one call insert_tar_target() / (): RStudio addin insert target skeletons

title: Simple utilities for working in targets
code: # After running tar_make(), load everything at once: tl()  # Equivalent to running: targets::tar_load_globals(envir = .GlobalEnv) targets::tar_load_everything(envir = .GlobalEnv)

what: Make and load a single target with tml()
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
code: # Make and immediately load the 'processed_data' target tml(processed_data)  # Equivalent to: targets::tar_make(processed_data) targets::tar_load(names = processed_data, envir = .GlobalEnv)

title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: insert_tar_target() function (aliased ()) RStudio addin inserts tar_target() skeleton cursor: especially useful building target lists:
code: # In an R script, position your cursor where you want the skeleton # Then run in the console: it()  # Or use Addins menu: Addins > Insert tar_target  # Inserts: # tar_target( #    name = , #    command =  #  ), tar_script({   list(     tar_target(name = raw_data, command = read.csv("data.csv")),     # Position cursor here and run it() to insert the next target skeleton   ) })

what: Handling nested spatial data with tar_terra_nested()
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: geotargets package handles terra::SpatRaster terra::SpatVector objects, serialize lists containing nested spatial objects. tar_terra_nested() fills gap recursively discovering managing nested spatial formats within arbitrary R objects.

previous_headings: Handling nested spatial data with tar_terra_nested()
what: Example: Nested spatial data in a list
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
code: library(targets)  tar_dir({    # tar_dir() runs code from a temporary directory.    tar_script({      library(targets)     library(geotargets)     library(terra)     library(tibble)     library(targets.utils)      # Create a function that produces a nested object with spatial data     make_nested_object <- function() {        r <- terra::rast(         system.file("ex", "elev.tif", package = "terra")       )        v <- terra::vect(         system.file("ex", "lux.shp", package = "terra")       )        # Return a list with nested SpatRaster and SpatVector objects       list(         raster = r,         vector = v,         metadata = tibble(           dataset = "example",           n_cells = terra::ncell(r),           n_features = nrow(v)         )       )     }      list(       # Use tar_terra_nested() just like tar_target() to handle the list       tar_terra_nested(         nested_spatial,         make_nested_object()       )     )   })    tar_make()    # Load and inspect the nested spatial object   x <- tar_read(nested_spatial)   x  }) #> terra 1.9.38 #> + nested_spatial dispatched #> ✔ nested_spatial completed [12ms, 46.45 kB] #> ✔ ended pipeline [220ms, 1 completed, 0 skipped] #> $raster #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 90, 95, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.008333333, 0.008333333  (x, y) #> extent      : 5.741667, 6.533333, 49.44167, 50.19167  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) #> source      : rast_000001.tif #> name        : elevation #> min value   :       141 #> max value   :       547 #>  #> $vector #> class       : SpatVector #> geometry    : polygons #> dimensions  : 12, 6  (geometries, attributes) #> extent      : 5.74414, 6.528252, 49.44781, 50.18162  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> source      : vect_000002.gpkg #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) #> names       :  ID_1   NAME_1  ID_2   NAME_2  AREA   POP #> type        : <num>    <chr> <num>    <chr> <num> <num> #> values      :     1 Diekirch     1 Clervaux   312 18081 #>                   1 Diekirch     2 Diekirch   218 32543 #>                   1 Diekirch     3  Redange   259 18664 #>               ... #>  #> $metadata #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   dataset n_cells n_features #> * <chr>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 example    8550         12

previous_headings: Handling nested spatial data with tar_terra_nested()
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: targets package stores objects RDS files default, serialize terra::SpatRaster terra::SpatVector objects. geotargets package solves top-level spatial objects using custom formats write GeoTIFF GeoPackage files. However, geotargets handle objects contain nested spatial formats, : Lists spatial elements Data frames spatial list-columns Nested lists mixing spatial non-spatial data S4 objects spatial slot data tar_terra_nested() works : Recursively scanning object SpatRaster SpatVector objects Writing individual files (GeoTIFF rasters, GeoPackage vectors) Replacing lightweight reference objects skeleton Compressing skeleton + spatial files single .tar.gz archive Reconstructing object restoring spatial files read

previous_headings: Handling nested spatial data with tar_terra_nested()
what: Advanced: Custom file formats and options
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: can customize spatial data written:
code: tar_script({   list(     tar_terra_nested(       my_target,       make_complex_nested_object(),       # Use Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF for rasters       raster_filetype = "COG",       # Custom raster creation options       raster_gdal = c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "PREDICTOR=2"),       # Use shapefile instead of GeoPackage for vectors       vector_filetype = "ESRI SHAPEFILE",       # Pass additional options to terra functions       raster_args = list(NAflag = -9999),       vector_args = list(layer = "myshapes")     )   ) })

previous_headings: Handling nested spatial data with tar_terra_nested()
what: Example: Nested spatial data in a tibble
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: can even use tar_terra_nested() tibbles containing spatial list-columns:
code: tar_script({   library(targets)   library(terra)   library(tibble)   library(targets.utils)    make_spatial_tibble <- function() {     r1 <- terra::rast(system.file("ex", "elev.tif", package = "terra"))     r2 <- terra::rast(system.file("ex", "elev.tif", package = "terra"))          tibble(       id = c("location_a", "location_b"),       raster = list(r1, r2),       description = c("Elevation at A", "Elevation at B")     )   }    list(     tar_terra_nested(       spatial_tibble,       make_spatial_tibble()     )   ) })

title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: ’s realistic example combining multiple targets.utils functions: interactively:
code: tar_script({   library(targets)   library(targets.utils)   library(terra)   library(tibble)    # A helper function that produces nested spatial data   process_spatial_data <- function(raster_file, vector_file) {     list(       raster = terra::rast(raster_file),       vector = terra::vect(vector_file),       processing_date = Sys.Date()     )   }    list(     # Load raw data     tar_target(       raw_spatial,       process_spatial_data(         system.file("ex", "elev.tif", package = "terra"),         system.file("ex", "lux.shp", package = "terra")       ),       # Use tar_terra_nested to handle the nested list       format = "custom_format"     ),          # Use it() here to insert the next target skeleton     # Cursor position + it() generates: tar_target(name = , command = ),   ) }) # After editing your _targets.R: tml(raw_spatial)  # Make and load raw_spatial target  # Inspect the loaded data str(raw_spatial)  # When done with all targets: tl()  # Load everything into your session

what: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
text: format recursively searches R object nested [terra::SpatRaster-class] [terra::SpatVector-class] objects.

title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
code: tar_format_terra_nested(   raster_filetype = "GTiff",   raster_gdal = NULL,   raster_datatype = NULL,   vector_filetype = "GPKG",   vector_gdal = NULL,   raster_args = list(),   vector_args = list() )

title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
text: raster_filetype Character length 1. GDAL raster driver passed [terra::writeRaster()]. Defaults `"GTiff"`. raster_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL raster creation options passed [terra::writeRaster()] `gdal` argument. raster_datatype Character length 1 `NULL`. Optional datatype passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_filetype Character length 1. GDAL vector driver passed [terra::writeVector()]. Defaults `"GPKG"`. vector_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL vector creation options passed [terra::writeVector()] `options` argument. raster_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeVector()].

title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
text: custom target format object [targets::tar_format()].

what: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
text: `tar_terra_nested()` target factory objects contain nested [terra::SpatRaster-class] [terra::SpatVector-class] objects top-level terra objects. Examples include lists, nested lists, tibbles list-columns, many ordinary serialisable R objects.

title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
code: tar_terra_nested(   name,   command,   pattern = NULL,   raster_filetype = "GTiff",   raster_gdal = NULL,   raster_datatype = NULL,   vector_filetype = "GPKG",   vector_gdal = NULL,   raster_args = list(),   vector_args = list(),   packages = targets::tar_option_get("packages"),   library = targets::tar_option_get("library"),   repository = targets::tar_option_get("repository"),   error = targets::tar_option_get("error"),   memory = targets::tar_option_get("memory"),   garbage_collection = targets::tar_option_get("garbage_collection"),   deployment = targets::tar_option_get("deployment"),   priority = targets::tar_option_get("priority"),   resources = targets::tar_option_get("resources"),   storage = targets::tar_option_get("storage"),   retrieval = targets::tar_option_get("retrieval"),   cue = targets::tar_option_get("cue"),   description = targets::tar_option_get("description") )

title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
text: name Symbol. Name target. command R code returning object may contain nested `SpatRaster` /`SpatVector` objects. pattern Code define dynamic branching pattern. See [targets::tar_target()]. raster_filetype Character length 1. GDAL raster driver passed [terra::writeRaster()]. Defaults `"GTiff"`. raster_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL raster creation options passed [terra::writeRaster()] `gdal` argument. raster_datatype Character length 1 `NULL`. Optional datatype passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_filetype Character length 1. GDAL vector driver passed [terra::writeVector()]. Defaults `"GPKG"`. vector_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL vector creation options passed [terra::writeVector()] `options` argument. raster_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeVector()]. packages Character vector packages load target. library Character vector library paths. repository Character length 1. Storage repository target. error Character length 1. Error handling strategy. memory Character length 1. Memory strategy. garbage_collection Logical. Whether run garbage collection. deployment Character length 1. Deployment strategy. priority Numeric length 1. Target priority. resources Object returned [targets::tar_resources()]. storage Character length 1. Storage strategy. retrieval Character length 1. Retrieval strategy. cue Object returned [targets::tar_cue()]. description Character length 1. Target description.

title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
text: target object use `targets` pipeline.
